No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY'S Motion For Entry Of Consent Judgment is granted. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Continued To Dec-09-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Sep-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT B. HANCOCK, ESQ. of Pacific Justice Center, Tel No. 415-310-1940 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ROBERT L. FALK, ESQ. of Morrison & Foerster LLP, Tel No. 415-268-6294 appearing for Defendant Artisan Confections Company and Dagoba Organic Chocolates LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS ARTISAN CONFECTIONS COMPANY AND DAGOBA ORGANIC CHOCOLATES LLC'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons, Defendants' Motion to Quash Service of Summons is DENIED. Where the degree of control exercise by a parent corporate entity is pervasive and continual, the parent may be deemed subject to local jurisdiction for acts of its subsidiary under agency principals. (Rollins Burdick Hunder of Southern California, Inc. v. Alexander Service, Inc. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 1, 9.) The actions and contacts of a parent corporate entity likewise may be attributable to a subsidiary for purposes of personal jurisdiction. (See Mathes v. National Utility Helicopters, Ltd. (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 182.) Provided that D Artisan retains intellectual property rights over D Dagoba chocolate, and the Dagoba chocolate that allegedly contains cadmium is sold in San Francisco stores - and has a retail website that provides a local San Francisco phone number and address for customer service - this court's exercise of personal jurisdiction over Defendants is proper. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT INCLUDES ADDITIONAL FACTORS DISCUSSED AT HEARING. PROPOSED ORDER SHALL BE SUBMITTED ADDITIONALLY IN WORD FORMAT SO THAT THE COURT MAY MAKE CHANGES TO THE PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
